title: Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled

seo.title: Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled: Authentic Indian Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of Tandoori Chicken charcoal grilled at home! This authentic Indian recipe delivers smoky, tender chicken with rich flavors. Perfect for dinner.

authentic Tandoori Chicken recipe charcoal grill
best Tandoori Chicken charcoal grill recipe
easy Tandoori Chicken charcoal grilled at home

Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: Close-up of perfectly charred Tandoori Chicken pieces on a charcoal grill, garnished with cilantro.
seo.imageCaption: Experience the smoky, authentic flavors of homemade Tandoori Chicken, perfectly grilled over charcoal.

description: Indulge in the smoky, vibrant flavors of authentic Tandoori Chicken, expertly grilled over charcoal. This classic Indian dish features succulent chicken marinated in a rich blend of yogurt and spices, resulting in tender, charred perfection. Prepare to delight your senses with this beloved culinary masterpiece, perfect for a memorable dinner.

instructions.title: Prepare the Chicken
instructions.text: Wash the chicken pieces thoroughly and pat them completely dry with paper towels. Make deep gashes on each piece with a knife, ensuring they penetrate the meat to allow the marinade to soak in. This is crucial for tender and flavorful Tandoori Chicken.

instructions.title: First Marinade
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with ginger garlic paste, lemon juice, salt, Kashmiri red chili powder, and turmeric powder. Rub this mixture well into all the chicken pieces, ensuring the gashes are filled. Let it marinate for at least 30 minutes at room temperature, or up to 2 hours in the refrigerator.

instructions.title: Second Marinade (Yogurt Base)
instructions.text: In a separate bowl, whisk together the thick yogurt, cumin powder, coriander powder, garam masala, mustard oil, and a pinch of red food color if using. Add this mixture to the chicken pieces, ensuring each piece is thoroughly coated. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for a minimum of 4 hours, preferably overnight, for best results.

instructions.title: Prepare the Charcoal Grill
instructions.text: Set up your charcoal grill for direct medium-high heat. Allow the charcoal to burn down until it is ash-covered and glowing red, indicating consistent heat. Lightly oil the grill grates to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Grill the Chicken
instructions.text: Place the marinated chicken pieces directly over the hot coals. Grill for about 5-7 minutes per side, turning occasionally, until the chicken is lightly charred and cooked through. The internal temperature should reach 165F (74C). Baste the chicken regularly with melted butter or ghee during the last 10 minutes of cooking to keep it moist and add flavor.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Once cooked, remove the Tandoori Chicken from the grill. Sprinkle generously with chaat masala, garnish with fresh coriander leaves, and serve immediately with lemon wedges, mint chutney, and sliced onions. Enjoy the authentic smoky flavors!

tips: For the best Tandoori Chicken, always use thick, drained yogurt (like Greek yogurt) as a watery marinade will not cling to the chicken properly.
Making deep cuts in the chicken pieces is essential; it allows the marinade to penetrate deeply, ensuring the chicken is flavorful and cooks evenly.
When grilling, do not overcrowd the grill. Cook in batches if necessary to maintain consistent heat and ensure proper charring and cooking.
article:
Mastering the art of Indian cuisine at home often feels like a daunting task, but some dishes, while seemingly complex, are incredibly rewarding and surprisingly achievable. Among them, Tandoori Chicken stands out as a global favorite, renowned for its vibrant color, smoky aroma, and tender, flavorful meat. When prepared on a charcoal grill, this classic Indian dinner recipe reaches new heights of authenticity, bringing the essence of a traditional tandoor right to your backyard. Get ready to impress your family and friends with this show-stopping dish that’s perfect for any occasion.

## What is Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled?

Tandoori Chicken is a quintessential Indian dish originating from the Punjab region. It involves chicken marinated in a mixture of yogurt and a blend of aromatic spices, then traditionally cooked at very high temperatures in a tandoor, a cylindrical clay oven. The intense heat of the tandoor cooks the chicken quickly, sealing in juices while imparting a distinctive smoky flavor and a slightly charred exterior.

When we talk about Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled, we're essentially replicating this traditional cooking method using a charcoal grill. The charcoal imparts a similar smoky depth and char that is difficult to achieve with conventional ovens or gas grills. The yogurt-based marinade tenderizes the chicken, while spices like ginger, garlic, cumin, coriander, and Kashmiri chili powder (which gives it its characteristic reddish hue without excessive heat) infuse it with incredible flavor. The result is succulent, juicy chicken with a tantalizing smoky finish – an absolute must-try for any lover of Indian food.

## Ingredients You Need

To create the perfect Tandoori Chicken, fresh, high-quality ingredients are key. Here’s what you’ll need:

For the Chicken:
*   1 whole chicken (approx. 3-4 lbs), cut into 8-10 pieces (or 6-8 chicken thighs/drumsticks)
*   2 tbsp lemon juice
*   1 tsp salt (for initial rub)

For the Marinade:
*   1 cup full-fat plain yogurt (Greek yogurt works well for a thicker marinade)
*   2 tbsp ginger-garlic paste (equal parts fresh ginger and garlic, crushed)
*   2 tbsp Tandoori masala powder (store-bought or homemade blend)
*   1 tbsp Kashmiri chili powder (for color and mild heat)
*   1 tsp turmeric powder
*   1 tsp ground cumin
*   1 tsp ground coriander
*   1/2 tsp black pepper
*   1/2 tsp red chili powder (optional, for extra heat)
*   1 tbsp mustard oil or vegetable oil (for marinade)
*   Salt to taste

For Basting and Garnish:
*   2 tbsp melted butter or oil (for basting)
*   Fresh coriander leaves, chopped (for garnish)
*   Lemon wedges (for serving)
*   Sliced red onions (for serving)

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Follow these steps to create an unforgettable Tandoori Chicken experience:

1.  **Prepare the Chicken:** Wash the chicken pieces thoroughly and pat them completely dry with paper towels. Using a sharp knife, make deep gashes (2-3 per piece) on the chicken, especially on the thicker parts. This allows the marinade to penetrate deeply.
2.  **First Marinade (Optional but Recommended):** In a bowl, rub the chicken pieces with 2 tablespoons of lemon juice and 1 teaspoon of salt. Let it rest for 15-20 minutes. This initial step helps tenderize the chicken and adds a layer of flavor.
3.  **Prepare the Main Marinade:** In a large bowl, combine the yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, Tandoori masala powder, Kashmiri chili powder, turmeric powder, ground cumin, ground coriander, black pepper, optional red chili powder, mustard oil, and salt. Mix everything until you have a smooth, well-combined paste.
4.  **Marinate the Chicken:** Add the chicken pieces to the marinade bowl. Using your hands (gloves recommended to avoid staining), thoroughly coat each piece of chicken with the marinade, ensuring it gets into all the gashes.
5.  **Refrigerate:** Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for a minimum of 4 hours, or ideally, overnight (12-24 hours). The longer the marination, the more flavorful and tender your Tandoori Chicken will be.
6.  **Preheat the Charcoal Grill:** Prepare your charcoal grill for medium-high heat. Arrange the charcoal on one side for direct grilling and leave some space on the other for indirect cooking, if needed. The grill grates should be hot. Clean and lightly oil the grates to prevent sticking.
7.  **Grill the Chicken:** Remove the chicken from the refrigerator about 30 minutes before grilling to bring it closer to room temperature. Scrape off any excess marinade from the chicken pieces to prevent burning. Place the chicken pieces directly over the hot coals.
8.  **Cook and Baste:** Grill the chicken for about 5-7 minutes per side, turning frequently to ensure even cooking and prevent charring too quickly. As the chicken cooks, baste it occasionally with melted butter or oil. Continue grilling for a total of 20-30 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through, the juices run clear, and an internal temperature of 165°F (74°C) is reached. The chicken should have beautiful char marks and a slightly crisp exterior.
9.  **Rest and Serve:** Once cooked, remove the Tandoori Chicken from the grill and let it rest for 5-10 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute, resulting in more tender meat. Garnish with fresh coriander, lemon wedges, and sliced red onions.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving restaurant-quality Tandoori Chicken at home is all about paying attention to a few key details:

*   **Quality Chicken:** Use fresh, good-quality chicken. Bone-in, skin-on pieces are traditional and offer the best flavor and moisture retention, but boneless thighs also work well.
*   **Deep Gashes:** Don't skip making those deep cuts! They are crucial for the marinade to penetrate the meat, ensuring flavor in every bite.
*   **Full-Fat Yogurt:** Opt for full-fat plain yogurt or Greek yogurt. The fat helps tenderize the chicken and prevents the marinade from curdling or separating during cooking.
*   **Marination Time is Key:** This cannot be stressed enough. A minimum of 4 hours is essential, but an overnight marination (12-24 hours) will yield significantly more flavorful and tender chicken.
*   **Don't Overcrowd the Grill:** Cook the chicken in batches if necessary. Overcrowding lowers the grill temperature and steams the chicken instead of grilling it, preventing a good char.
*   **Manage Grill Heat:** Keep an eye on your charcoal grill's temperature. If the chicken starts to char too quickly, move it to a cooler part of the grill or raise the grill grate.
*   **Resting Time:** Always rest the chicken after grilling. This allows the muscle fibers to relax and reabsorb the juices, making the chicken incredibly tender and moist.

## Serving Suggestions

Tandoori Chicken is a star dish that deserves equally delicious accompaniments to complete the Indian dinner experience.

*   **Breads:** Serve hot with freshly baked naan bread, roti, or paratha to scoop up the flavorful chicken and any accompanying sauces.
*   **Rice:** A side of fragrant Basmati rice, Jeera rice (cumin rice), or even a simple pilaf complements the rich flavors beautifully.
*   **Chutneys and Dips:** A cooling mint-coriander chutney or a refreshing raita (yogurt dip with cucumber and spices) provides a perfect counterpoint to the smoky, spicy chicken.
*   **Salad:** A simple kachumber salad (diced cucumber, tomato, onion with lemon and spices) adds freshness and crunch.
*   **Lemon and Onion:** Always serve with extra lemon wedges for squeezing over the chicken and thin slices of red onion, which add a sharp, refreshing bite.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Tandoori Chicken is hard to beat, there are several ways to adapt this recipe to your preferences or dietary needs:

*   **Chicken Parts:** If you prefer boneless, skinless chicken, use thighs or breasts. Adjust grilling time accordingly (boneless pieces will cook faster).
*   **Spice Level:** Adjust the amount of red chili powder to control the heat. For a milder flavor, omit it entirely. For more heat, add a pinch more.
*   **Homemade Tandoori Masala:** If you can't find Tandoori masala, you can make a simplified version by combining ground coriander, cumin, turmeric, garam masala, ginger powder, garlic powder, and a pinch of fenugreek leaves.
*   **Other Cooking Methods:** While charcoal grilling is ideal for that authentic smoky flavor, you can also cook Tandoori Chicken in an oven (on a high heat setting, 450°F/230°C, using a broiler pan) or even an air fryer (at 375°F/190°C). Just remember that the smoky essence will be less pronounced.
*   **Vegetarian Options:** The same marinade can be used for delicious vegetarian alternatives like paneer tikka (cubes of Indian cheese), mushroom tikka, or even firm tofu. Adjust cooking times as needed.

Bringing the vibrant flavors of India to your dinner table has never been easier or more rewarding. This Tandoori Chicken charcoal grilled recipe offers an authentic taste that will transport your senses straight to the bustling streets of Delhi or the serene beaches of Goa. With a little preparation and these detailed instructions, you’ll be serving up a masterpiece that’s not just a meal, but an experience. So fire up your grill, gather your ingredients, and get ready to savor the incredible taste of homemade Tandoori Chicken!
